portraiture.en: the art or practice of creating portraits of people

ex.en: The exhibition celebrates two centuries of British portraiture.
ex.ko: 그 전시회는 2세기에 걸친 영국 초상화의 역사를 기린다.

posit.en: put forward an idea as a basis for argument or discussion

ex.en: The theory posits that memory is reconstructed rather than replayed.
ex.ko: 그 이론은 기억이 그대로 재생되는 것이 아니라 재구성된다고 상정한다.

posterity.en: all the people who will live in the future

ex.en: She kept a detailed diary for posterity.
ex.ko: 그녀는 후세를 위해 상세한 일기를 남겼다.

postural.en: relating to the position in which someone holds their body

ex.en: Sitting all day can lead to postural problems in the spine.
ex.ko: 하루 종일 앉아 있으면 척추에 자세 관련 문제가 생길 수 있다.

posturing.en: behavior intended to impress or mislead others rather than reflect true feelings

ex.en: His tough talk was dismissed as mere posturing.
ex.ko: 그의 강경한 발언은 단순한 허세로 치부되었다.

practitioner.en: a person who works in a particular profession, especially medicine or law

ex.en: She is a qualified medical practitioner.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자격을 갖춘 의료 종사자이다.

precocious.en: showing mature abilities or characteristics at an unusually early age

ex.en: He was a precocious child who could read at the age of three.
ex.ko: 그는 세 살에 글을 읽을 수 있었던 조숙한 아이였다.

precociously.en: in a way that shows unusually early development

ex.en: She spoke precociously about politics for a ten-year-old.
ex.ko: 그녀는 열 살치고는 조숙하게 정치 이야기를 했다.

preconception.en: an idea or opinion formed before you have enough information or experience

ex.en: Try to visit the country without any preconceptions.
ex.ko: 선입견 없이 그 나라를 방문해 보세요.

predilection.en: a strong natural liking for something

ex.en: He has a predilection for spicy food.
ex.ko: 그는 매운 음식을 특히 좋아한다.

preeminent.en: surpassing all others in a particular field

ex.en: She is the preeminent expert in this field.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이 분야에서 가장 탁월한 전문가이다.

preoccupation.en: a state of being completely absorbed in a particular thought or subject

ex.en: His main preoccupation was with his career.
ex.ko: 그의 가장 큰 관심사는 자신의 커리어였다.

prescriptive.en: stating what should be done, rather than describing what actually happens

ex.en: Grammar books are often criticized for being too prescriptive.
ex.ko: 문법책은 지나치게 규범적이라는 비판을 종종 받는다.

presentational.en: relating to the way something is presented or shown

ex.en: The teacher gave feedback on the presentational style of the report.
ex.ko: 선생님은 보고서의 표현 방식에 대한 피드백을 주었다.

preservationist.en: a person who works to protect historical sites, buildings, or the natural environment

ex.en: Preservationists fought to save the old theater from demolition.
ex.ko: 보존 운동가들은 그 오래된 극장을 철거로부터 지키기 위해 싸웠다.

pressurization.en: the process of keeping the air pressure inside something, such as an aircraft cabin, at a controlled level

ex.en: The plane lost pressurization at high altitude.
ex.ko: 그 비행기는 고도에서 기압 조절 기능을 상실했다.

pressurize.en: increase or maintain the air pressure inside something

ex.en: The cabin is pressurized to a safe altitude equivalent.
ex.ko: 객실은 안전한 고도에 맞춰 가압된다.

pressurize.en: (British) force or persuade someone to do something

ex.en: She was pressurized into signing the contract.
ex.ko: 그녀는 계약서에 서명하도록 강요당했다.

presuppose.en: assume something to be true before it has been proven, or require it as a necessary condition

ex.en: His argument presupposes that everyone has equal access to education.
ex.ko: 그의 주장은 모든 사람이 교육에 동등하게 접근할 수 있다는 것을 전제로 한다.

presupposition.en: something assumed to be true before an argument or discussion is made

ex.en: The plan is based on a presupposition that may not be valid.
ex.ko: 그 계획은 타당하지 않을 수도 있는 전제에 기반한다.

primeval.en: belonging to the earliest times; ancient and primitive

ex.en: The explorers ventured into a primeval forest untouched by humans.
ex.ko: 탐험가들은 사람의 손이 닿지 않은 원시림 속으로 들어갔다.

prise.en: (British) use force to open or move something, especially with a tool as a lever

ex.en: He prised the lid off the paint can with a screwdriver.
ex.ko: 그는 드라이버로 페인트 통 뚜껑을 비틀어 열었다.

prissy.en: excessively proper, neat, or easily shocked by anything considered improper

ex.en: She gave a prissy little cough when he swore.
ex.ko: 그가 욕을 하자 그녀는 얌전 빼며 헛기침을 했다.

procedural.en: relating to the way something is done rather than the content itself

ex.en: The case was dismissed on procedural grounds.
ex.ko: 그 사건은 절차상의 이유로 기각되었다.

profitability.en: the degree to which a business makes a profit

ex.en: The new strategy improved the company's profitability.
ex.ko: 그 새로운 전략은 회사의 수익성을 향상시켰다.

proliferation.en: a rapid increase in the number or amount of something

ex.en: The report warns about the proliferation of nuclear weapons.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 핵무기의 확산에 대해 경고한다.

ex.en: The treatment can prolong the patient's life by several years.
ex.ko: 그 치료는 환자의 생명을 몇 년 더 연장할 수 있다.

promenade.en: a paved public path, especially along a seafront, for walking

ex.en: They strolled along the promenade watching the sunset.
ex.ko: 그들은 노을을 보며 산책로를 따라 걸었다.

promenade.en: take a leisurely walk, especially in a public place to be seen

ex.en: Couples promenaded along the boardwalk in the evening.
ex.ko: 저녁이 되자 커플들이 산책로를 따라 거닐었다.

promotable.en: suitable or ready to be given a higher position or rank

ex.en: He was seen as promotable within the first year.
ex.ko: 그는 입사 첫해부터 승진 가능한 인재로 여겨졌다.

prone.en: likely to suffer from or do something, usually something bad

ex.en: He is prone to making careless mistakes.
ex.ko: 그는 부주의한 실수를 하기 쉽다.

ex.en: The patient was lying prone on the examination table.
ex.ko: 그 환자는 진찰대 위에 엎드려 누워 있었다.

proportional.en: corresponding in size or amount to something else

ex.en: Her salary increase was proportional to her years of experience.
ex.ko: 그녀의 급여 인상은 근무 연수에 비례했다.

proportionally.en: in a way that corresponds proportionally in size or amount

ex.en: Taxes should rise proportionally with income.
ex.ko: 세금은 소득에 비례하여 올라야 한다.

proposition.en: a suggestion or plan put forward for consideration

ex.en: The company received an interesting business proposition.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 흥미로운 사업 제안을 받았다.

proposition.en: a statement that can be judged true or false

ex.en: The mathematician proved the proposition using a simple proof.
ex.ko: 그 수학자는 간단한 증명으로 그 명제를 증명했다.

proprietary.en: protected by trademark, patent, or copyright and owned by a particular company

ex.en: The software uses a proprietary file format.
ex.ko: 그 소프트웨어는 독점적인 파일 형식을 사용한다.

proprietorial.en: behaving as if you own or control something

ex.en: He gave a proprietorial nod toward the project he had led.
ex.ko: 그는 자신이 주도한 프로젝트를 향해 소유주 같은 태도로 고개를 끄덕였다.

proscribe.en: forbid something, especially by law or official rule

ex.en: The law proscribes the use of certain pesticides.
ex.ko: 그 법은 특정 살충제 사용을 금지한다.

proscription.en: an official rule or decree that forbids something

ex.en: The treaty includes a proscription against chemical weapons.
ex.ko: 그 조약에는 화학 무기에 대한 금지 조항이 포함되어 있다.

prostrate.en: lying flat on the ground, especially face down

ex.en: He lay prostrate on the floor in front of the shrine.
ex.ko: 그는 사당 앞 바닥에 납작 엎드려 누웠다.

prostrate.en: physically or emotionally overwhelmed and drained of strength

ex.en: She was left prostrate with grief after the funeral.
ex.ko: 그녀는 장례식 후 슬픔에 못 이겨 기진맥진했다.

prostrate.en: lie face down on the ground, especially as a sign of submission or respect

ex.en: The monks prostrated themselves before the altar.
ex.ko: 수도승들은 제단 앞에 엎드렸다.

protrude.en: stick out or extend beyond a surface

ex.en: His front teeth protrude slightly.
ex.ko: 그는 앞니가 살짝 튀어나와 있다.

protrusion.en: a part that sticks out from a surface

ex.en: The X-ray revealed a small protrusion in the disc.
ex.ko: 엑스레이에서 디스크의 작은 돌출부가 발견되었다.

proverbial.en: used to refer to something well known, often as if from a proverb

ex.en: He was the proverbial fish out of water at the formal dinner.
ex.ko: 그는 그 격식 있는 만찬에서 흔히 말하는 물 밖에 나온 물고기 신세였다.

provincial.en: relating to a province or the regions outside the capital city

ex.en: She grew up in a small provincial town.
ex.ko: 그녀는 작은 지방 도시에서 자랐다.

provincial.en: having a narrow view of the world, typical of someone unfamiliar with wider culture

ex.en: His provincial attitudes surprised his well-traveled colleagues.
ex.ko: 그의 편협한 태도는 세계 각지를 다녀본 동료들을 놀라게 했다.

provocation.en: an action or remark that makes someone angry, often deliberately

ex.en: He remained calm despite the provocation.
ex.ko: 그는 도발에도 불구하고 침착함을 유지했다.

proximal.en: situated nearest to the center of the body or the point of attachment

ex.en: The proximal end of the bone connects to the joint.
ex.ko: 그 뼈의 근위쪽 끝은 관절에 연결된다.

prurience.en: an excessive interest in sexual matters

ex.en: The tabloid was criticized for its prurience.
ex.ko: 그 타블로이드지는 음란한 관심사만 다룬다는 비판을 받았다.

prurient.en: showing too much interest in sexual matters

ex.en: The article was criticized for its prurient tone.
ex.ko: 그 기사는 음란한 논조로 비판받았다.

ex.en: The court imposed punitive damages on the company.
ex.ko: 법원은 그 회사에 징벌적 손해배상을 부과했다.

punitively.en: in a way that is intended to punish

ex.en: The tax was applied punitively to discourage the practice.
ex.ko: 그 관행을 억제하기 위해 세금이 징벌적으로 부과되었다.

puritanical.en: having very strict moral or religious views, especially about pleasure and sex

ex.en: Her puritanical upbringing made her uncomfortable with the party's atmosphere.
ex.ko: 그녀는 청교도적인 가정교육 탓에 그 파티 분위기가 불편했다.

pyre.en: a pile of wood for burning a dead body as part of a funeral

ex.en: The body was placed on a funeral pyre and set alight.
ex.ko: 시신은 화장용 장작더미 위에 놓인 뒤 불태워졌다.

qualitative.en: relating to the quality or nature of something rather than to numbers or amounts

ex.en: The researchers used qualitative interviews rather than surveys.
ex.ko: 연구자들은 설문 대신 정성적 인터뷰를 사용했다.

qualm.en: a feeling of doubt or unease about whether an action is right

ex.en: She had no qualms about telling him the truth.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그에게 사실을 말하는 데 아무 거리낌이 없었다.

ex.en: Her lips quivered as she tried not to cry.
ex.ko: 그녀는 울지 않으려 애쓰며 입술을 떨었다.

quiver.en: a slight shaking movement or feeling

ex.en: A quiver of excitement ran through the crowd.
ex.ko: 군중 사이로 흥분의 떨림이 퍼졌다.

ex.en: The archer pulled an arrow from his quiver.
ex.ko: 궁수는 화살통에서 화살 하나를 뽑았다.

rapture.en: a feeling of intense pleasure or joy

ex.en: The audience listened in rapture as she sang.
ex.ko: 관객들은 그녀의 노래를 황홀경에 빠져 들었다.

rapturous.en: showing great pleasure, joy, or enthusiasm

ex.en: The band received a rapturous welcome from the fans.
ex.ko: 그 밴드는 팬들로부터 열광적인 환영을 받았다.

rapturously.en: in a way that shows intense pleasure or enthusiasm

ex.en: The critics praised the performance rapturously.
ex.ko: 평론가들은 그 공연을 열광적으로 극찬했다.

ex.en: Visitors are ravished by the beauty of the coastline.
ex.ko: 방문객들은 그 해안선의 아름다움에 넋을 빼앗긴다.

ex.en: The ball rebounded off the wall.
ex.ko: 그 공은 벽에 맞고 다시 튀어올랐다.

rebound.en: recover quickly after a difficult period or setback

ex.en: The stock market rebounded after the initial drop.
ex.ko: 주식시장은 초기 하락 이후 반등했다.

rebound.en: an instance of improving or recovering after a decline or setback

ex.en: Sales showed a strong rebound in the last quarter.
ex.ko: 매출은 지난 분기에 강한 반등세를 보였다.

rebuke.en: criticize someone sharply for something they have done

ex.en: The manager rebuked him for arriving late again.
ex.ko: 관리자는 그가 또 늦게 온 것을 질책했다.

rebuke.en: a sharp expression of criticism or disapproval

ex.en: She accepted the rebuke without complaint.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 질책을 불평 없이 받아들였다.

recitation.en: the act of saying something aloud from memory, especially in front of an audience

ex.en: The students gave a recitation of the poem.
ex.ko: 학생들은 그 시를 암송했다.

recluse.en: a person who lives alone and avoids other people

ex.en: After the scandal, he became a recluse.
ex.ko: 그 스캔들 이후 그는 은둔자가 되었다.

reclusive.en: preferring to live alone and avoid other people

ex.en: The author lived a reclusive life in the countryside.
ex.ko: 그 작가는 시골에서 은둔하는 삶을 살았다.

reconcile.en: restore friendly relations between people after a disagreement

ex.en: The brothers finally reconciled after years apart.
ex.ko: 그 형제는 몇 년 만에 마침내 화해했다.

reconcile.en: make two seemingly opposing ideas or facts compatible with each other

ex.en: It's hard to reconcile his two stories.
ex.ko: 그의 두 가지 이야기를 서로 일치시키기는 어렵다.

reconciliation.en: the restoring of friendly relations after a disagreement

ex.en: The two countries reached a reconciliation after decades of conflict.
ex.ko: 그 두 나라는 수십 년간의 갈등 끝에 화해에 이르렀다.

recourse.en: a source of help that can be turned to in a difficult situation

ex.en: Without insurance, she had no recourse but to pay for the repairs herself.
ex.ko: 보험이 없어서 그녀는 직접 수리비를 지불하는 것 외에 다른 수단이 없었다.

recumbent.en: lying down, especially in a relaxed position

ex.en: He read the newspaper in a recumbent position on the sofa.
ex.ko: 그는 소파에 누운 자세로 신문을 읽었다.

recuperate.en: get back health or strength after an illness or injury

ex.en: She went to the countryside to recuperate after surgery.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수술 후 회복하기 위해 시골로 갔다.

recuperation.en: the gradual process of regaining health after illness or injury

ex.en: Full recuperation from the operation took several months.
ex.ko: 그 수술로부터의 완전한 회복은 몇 달이 걸렸다.

recuperative.en: helping someone to recover health or strength

ex.en: The spa is known for its recuperative powers.
ex.ko: 그 스파는 회복을 돕는 효과로 유명하다.

redundancy.en: the state of being unnecessary because it duplicates something else

ex.en: The editor removed several redundancies from the essay.
ex.ko: 편집자는 그 에세이에서 여러 불필요한 중복 표현을 삭제했다.

redundancy.en: (British) the loss of a job because it is no longer needed

ex.en: Hundreds of workers faced redundancy when the factory closed.
ex.ko: 그 공장이 문을 닫으면서 수백 명의 노동자가 정리해고를 당했다.

redundantly.en: in a way that unnecessarily repeats something already said or done

ex.en: The instructions were written redundantly, repeating the same warning three times.
ex.ko: 그 설명서는 같은 경고를 세 번이나 반복하며 불필요하게 중복 작성되었다.

regulatory.en: relating to the control of an activity by official rules

ex.en: The bank must comply with new regulatory requirements.
ex.ko: 그 은행은 새로운 규제 요건을 준수해야 한다.

reissue.en: publish or release something again, often with small changes

ex.en: The publisher decided to reissue the novel with a new cover.
ex.ko: 그 출판사는 새 표지로 그 소설을 재발행하기로 했다.

relegate.en: assign someone or something to a lower or less important position

ex.en: The star player was relegated to the bench after his injury.
ex.ko: 그 스타 선수는 부상 이후 후보 선수로 밀려났다.

remit.en: send money as payment to a place or person

ex.en: Workers abroad often remit money to their families.
ex.ko: 해외 근로자들은 종종 가족에게 돈을 송금한다.

remit.en: the tasks or subjects that a person or organization is responsible for

ex.en: Improving public transport falls within the committee's remit.
ex.ko: 대중교통 개선은 그 위원회의 직무 범위에 속한다.

remittance.en: an amount of money sent to someone, especially in another place

ex.en: The family relies on monthly remittances from abroad.
ex.ko: 그 가족은 해외에서 오는 매달의 송금액에 의지한다.

remonstrance.en: a strong expression of protest or objection

ex.en: Their remonstrance had no effect on the committee's decision.
ex.ko: 그들의 항의는 위원회의 결정에 아무 영향을 미치지 못했다.

remonstrate.en: argue or protest strongly against something

ex.en: She remonstrated with the referee over the decision.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 판정에 대해 심판에게 항의했다.

remorseful.en: feeling sorry and guilty for something wrong you have done

ex.en: He seemed genuinely remorseful about the accident.
ex.ko: 그는 그 사고에 대해 진심으로 뉘우치는 듯 보였다.

repose.en: a state of peaceful rest or freedom from activity

ex.en: She finally found a moment of repose after a hectic week.
ex.ko: 그녀는 정신없이 바쁜 한 주를 보낸 뒤 마침내 잠깐의 휴식을 찾았다.

repose.en: lie or rest in a comfortable position

ex.en: He reposed on the hammock all afternoon.
ex.ko: 그는 오후 내내 해먹에 누워 쉬었다.

reproach.en: express disapproval or disappointment toward someone for something they did

ex.en: She reproached him for forgetting her birthday.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그가 자기 생일을 잊었다고 책망했다.

reproach.en: an expression of disapproval or disappointment

ex.en: He looked at her with a hint of reproach in his eyes.
ex.ko: 그는 눈빛에 살짝 책망을 담아 그녀를 바라보았다.

ex.en: The lizard's reptilian eyes stared unblinking.
ex.ko: 그 도마뱀의 파충류다운 눈은 깜빡임 없이 응시했다.

requisition.en: a formal official request for something needed

ex.en: The department submitted a requisition for new computers.
ex.ko: 그 부서는 새 컴퓨터 구매 요청서를 제출했다.

requisition.en: officially demand and take something for public or military use

ex.en: The army requisitioned local vehicles during the crisis.
ex.ko: 군은 그 위기 동안 지역 차량들을 징발했다.

residual.en: remaining after most of something has been removed or used

ex.en: There was still a residual smell of smoke in the room.
ex.ko: 방 안에는 여전히 남은 담배 냄새가 있었다.

residue.en: a small amount of something that remains after the main part has gone or been used

ex.en: There was a sticky residue left on the counter.
ex.ko: 조리대 위에 끈적한 찌꺼기가 남아 있었다.

resound.en: fill a place with a loud, echoing sound

ex.en: Her laughter resounded through the hall.
ex.ko: 그녀의 웃음소리가 홀 전체에 울려 퍼졌다.

resounding.en: very great or complete, leaving no doubt

ex.en: The team celebrated a resounding victory.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 압도적인 승리를 자축했다.

resoundingly.en: in a way that is very clear or complete, leaving no doubt

ex.en: The proposal was resoundingly rejected.
ex.ko: 그 제안은 압도적으로 거부되었다.

resuscitate.en: bring someone back to consciousness after they have stopped breathing or their heart has stopped

ex.en: Paramedics managed to resuscitate the patient.
ex.ko: 응급 구조대원들은 그 환자를 소생시키는 데 성공했다.

resuscitation.en: the act of reviving someone who has stopped breathing or whose heart has stopped

ex.en: The lifeguard performed resuscitation on the swimmer.
ex.ko: 그 인명구조원은 그 수영객에게 심폐소생술을 실시했다.

retrospection.en: the act of looking back and thinking about past events

ex.en: In retrospection, the decision seems obvious.
ex.ko: 회고해 보면, 그 결정은 당연해 보인다.

retrospective.en: looking back on or dealing with past events

ex.en: She took a retrospective look at her career.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 커리어를 회고하는 시간을 가졌다.

retrospective.cue: 한 사람의 작품 인생을 총정리하는 전시
retrospective.en: an exhibition showing an artist's work over a long period of their career

ex.en: The museum is holding a retrospective of the painter's fifty-year career.
ex.ko: 그 미술관은 그 화가의 50년 화업을 아우르는 회고전을 열고 있다.

revelatory.en: revealing something previously unknown or hidden

ex.en: The interview was revelatory about his true motives.
ex.ko: 그 인터뷰는 그의 진짜 의도를 드러내 주었다.

reverb.en: the effect of sound continuing to be heard after it is produced, especially in music

ex.en: The guitarist added some reverb to make the sound richer.
ex.ko: 그 기타 연주자는 소리를 더 풍부하게 만들기 위해 잔향 효과를 더했다.

reverberate.en: (of sound) continue to be heard as it echoes

ex.en: The explosion reverberated through the valley.
ex.ko: 그 폭발음은 계곡 전체에 울려 퍼졌다.

ex.en: The scandal reverberated throughout the industry for years.
ex.ko: 그 스캔들은 몇 년 동안 업계 전체에 파장을 일으켰다.

reverberation.en: the repeated echoing of a sound

ex.en: The cave amplified every reverberation of their voices.
ex.ko: 그 동굴은 그들 목소리의 모든 울림을 증폭시켰다.

reverberation.en: an indirect effect or consequence of an event

ex.en: The decision had reverberations across the whole company.
ex.ko: 그 결정은 회사 전체에 걸쳐 파장을 남겼다.

revile.en: criticize someone or something in a harsh, abusive way

ex.en: He was reviled by the press for his comments.
ex.ko: 그는 그 발언 때문에 언론으로부터 매도당했다.

ex.en: She felt a wave of revulsion at the smell.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 냄새에 혐오감이 밀려왔다.

rhetoric.en: language designed to persuade or impress, sometimes with little real substance

ex.en: His speech was full of rhetoric but light on details.
ex.ko: 그의 연설은 미사여구로 가득했지만 구체적인 내용은 부족했다.

rhetorical.en: relating to language used for effect or persuasion rather than to convey real meaning

ex.en: "Who cares?" she said, but it was clearly a rhetorical question.
ex.ko: "누가 신경이나 쓴대?"라고 그녀는 말했지만, 그것은 분명 수사적인 질문이었다.

rhetorically.en: in a way relating to persuasive or stylistic language

ex.en: "Do I look like I'm joking?" he asked rhetorically.
ex.ko: "내가 농담하는 것처럼 보여?" 그는 수사적으로 물었다.

rickety.en: likely to collapse or break because it is old, weak, or badly made

ex.en: We climbed the rickety old staircase carefully.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 삐걱거리는 낡은 계단을 조심스럽게 올라갔다.

rife.en: very common, especially something bad or unpleasant

ex.en: Corruption was rife in the local government.
ex.ko: 그 지방 정부에는 부패가 만연했다.

rift.en: a serious disagreement that damages a relationship

ex.en: The argument caused a deep rift between the two friends.
ex.ko: 그 말다툼은 두 친구 사이에 깊은 불화를 일으켰다.

romp.en: play in a lively, energetic way

ex.en: The puppies romped around the yard all afternoon.
ex.ko: 강아지들은 오후 내내 마당에서 뛰놀았다.

romp.en: win a game or competition easily

ex.en: The home team romped to victory, 5-0.
ex.ko: 홈팀은 5대0으로 손쉽게 승리했다.

romper.en: a one-piece garment worn by babies or young children

ex.en: She dressed the baby in a cute yellow romper.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아기에게 귀여운 노란색 우주복을 입혔다.

ruminant.en: an animal, such as a cow or deer, that chews the cud and has a stomach with several chambers

ex.en: Cows are ruminants that digest grass in multiple stomachs.
ex.ko: 소는 여러 개의 위로 풀을 소화하는 반추동물이다.

ruminant.en: relating to or characteristic of animals that chew the cud

ex.en: Deer share the ruminant digestive system found in cattle.
ex.ko: 사슴은 소에게서 볼 수 있는 반추동물의 소화 체계를 공유한다.

ruminate.en: think carefully and deeply about something for a long time

ex.en: She sat by the window, ruminating on the day's events.
ex.ko: 그녀는 창가에 앉아 그날 있었던 일들을 곰곰이 생각했다.

rumination.en: the act of thinking deeply and carefully about something

ex.en: His journal was full of quiet ruminations on life.
ex.ko: 그의 일기는 삶에 대한 조용한 사색으로 가득했다.

ruminative.en: showing that someone is thinking deeply about something

ex.en: He gave a ruminative nod before answering.
ex.ko: 그는 사색에 잠긴 듯 고개를 끄덕인 뒤 대답했다.

ex.en: The salivary glands produce saliva to help digestion.
ex.ko: 타액선은 소화를 돕는 침을 분비한다.

sandwiched.en: placed tightly between two other things

ex.en: The small house was sandwiched between two tall office buildings.
ex.ko: 그 작은 집은 두 개의 높은 사무용 건물 사이에 끼여 있었다.

scavenger.en: an animal that feeds on dead or decaying matter

ex.en: Vultures are scavengers that feed on dead animals.
ex.ko: 독수리는 죽은 동물을 먹는 청소동물이다.

scavenger.en: a person who collects discarded items that others have thrown away

ex.en: The scavenger sorted through the trash looking for scrap metal.
ex.ko: 그 폐품 수집가는 고철을 찾아 쓰레기를 뒤졌다.

ex.en: She wrote a scholarly article on ancient Roman history.
ex.ko: 그녀는 고대 로마사에 관한 학술적인 논문을 썼다.

scroll.en: move text or images on a screen up or down to see more content

ex.en: Scroll down to see more photos.
ex.ko: 사진을 더 보려면 아래로 스크롤하세요.

scroll.en: a roll of paper or parchment with writing on it

ex.en: The ancient scroll was carefully preserved in the museum.
ex.ko: 그 고대 두루마리는 박물관에 조심스럽게 보관되어 있다.

secular.en: not connected with religion or spiritual matters

ex.en: The country has a secular government separate from religion.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 종교와 분리된 세속적인 정부를 갖고 있다.

secularly.en: in a way that is not connected with religion

ex.en: The holiday is celebrated secularly by many families.
ex.ko: 그 명절은 많은 가정에서 종교와 무관하게 기념된다.

seedy.en: shabby, dirty, and generally disreputable in appearance

ex.en: They stayed in a seedy motel on the edge of town.
ex.ko: 그들은 시 외곽의 허름한 모텔에 묵었다.

ex.en: The seedy watermelon was hard to eat neatly.
ex.ko: 그 씨가 많은 수박은 깔끔하게 먹기 어려웠다.

seismic.en: relating to earthquakes or ground vibrations

ex.en: The building was designed to withstand seismic activity.
ex.ko: 그 건물은 지진 활동을 견디도록 설계되었다.

seismic.en: having a huge and significant impact, like an earthquake

ex.en: The election result marked a seismic shift in politics.
ex.ko: 그 선거 결과는 정치계에 엄청난 변화를 가져왔다.

selectivity.en: the quality of being careful and selective in choosing something

ex.en: The university is known for its high selectivity in admissions.
ex.ko: 그 대학은 입학에서 까다로운 선발 기준으로 유명하다.

self-aggrandizement.en: the act of increasing your own power or making yourself seem important

ex.en: His speech was pure self-aggrandizement with little substance.
ex.ko: 그의 연설은 실속 없이 순전한 자기과시였다.

shamble.en: walk in a slow, awkward way, dragging your feet

ex.en: The old man shambled across the street.
ex.ko: 그 노인은 발을 끌며 길을 건넜다.

sheaf.en: a bundle of things, such as papers or grain stalks, tied together

ex.en: She carried a sheaf of documents under her arm.
ex.ko: 그녀는 서류 한 다발을 팔에 끼고 있었다.

shimmer.en: shine with a soft, faint, and slightly unsteady light

ex.en: The lake shimmered in the moonlight.
ex.ko: 그 호수는 달빛 아래 은은하게 반짝였다.

shoal.en: an area of shallow water, often with a sandbank, in a sea or river

ex.en: The boat ran aground on a shoal near the shore.
ex.ko: 그 배는 해안 근처의 모래톱에 좌초했다.

shoal.en: a large group of fish swimming together

ex.en: A shoal of sardines swam past the divers.
ex.ko: 정어리 떼가 잠수부들 옆을 헤엄쳐 지나갔다.

side-step.en: avoid dealing with a difficult question or issue, or physically move to the side to avoid something

ex.en: The politician sidestepped the reporter's question.
ex.ko: 그 정치인은 기자의 질문을 회피했다.

singe.en: burn something slightly on the surface

ex.en: She singed her hair while lighting the candles.
ex.ko: 그녀는 초에 불을 붙이다가 머리카락을 살짝 태웠다.

skew.en: make something unfairly biased or distorted in one direction

ex.en: The survey results were skewed by a small sample size.
ex.ko: 그 설문 결과는 작은 표본 크기 때문에 왜곡되었다.

skew.en: set at an angle; not straight or level

ex.en: The picture frame hung at a skew angle on the wall.
ex.ko: 그 액자는 벽에 비스듬한 각도로 걸려 있었다.

slab.en: a thick, flat piece of a hard material such as stone or concrete

ex.en: The patio was made of large stone slabs.
ex.ko: 그 테라스는 큼직한 돌판으로 만들어졌다.

slither.en: move smoothly along the ground with a twisting motion, like a snake

ex.en: The snake slithered silently through the grass.
ex.ko: 그 뱀은 풀숲을 소리 없이 미끄러지듯 나아갔다.

smattering.en: a small amount of something, especially a superficial knowledge of a subject

ex.en: He has only a smattering of French.
ex.ko: 그는 프랑스어를 약간밖에 모른다.

snippet.en: a small piece or extract of something, such as text or conversation

ex.en: I caught only a snippet of their conversation.
ex.ko: 나는 그들 대화의 짧은 부분만 들었다.

snobbery.en: the attitude of judging others based on social class, wealth, or taste, and feeling superior

ex.en: His snobbery made him unpopular with his coworkers.
ex.ko: 그의 속물근성 때문에 그는 동료들 사이에서 인기가 없었다.

solicit.en: ask for something, such as money, help, or an opinion, in a formal way

ex.en: The charity solicited donations from local businesses.
ex.ko: 그 자선단체는 지역 사업체들로부터 기부를 요청했다.

soliloquize.en: speak your thoughts aloud when alone, especially in a play

ex.en: The actor soliloquized about his fate in the final scene.
ex.ko: 그 배우는 마지막 장면에서 자신의 운명에 대해 독백했다.

solipsism.en: the philosophical idea that only your own mind is sure to exist

ex.en: His extreme solipsism made him doubt that other people had real feelings.
ex.ko: 그의 극단적인 유아론은 다른 사람들이 진짜 감정을 가지고 있다는 것조차 의심하게 만들었다.

ex.en: He grew to enjoy the solitariness of country life.
ex.ko: 그는 시골 생활의 고독함을 점점 즐기게 되었다.

solitary.en: done or existing alone, without other people

ex.en: She took a solitary walk along the beach.
ex.ko: 그녀는 해변을 따라 홀로 산책을 했다.

solitary.en: the punishment of keeping a prisoner alone, separate from others

ex.en: The inmate was put in solitary for fighting.
ex.ko: 그 수감자는 싸움을 벌여 독방에 감금되었다.

solitude.en: the state of being alone, often peacefully

ex.en: She enjoys the solitude of her mountain cabin.
ex.ko: 그녀는 산속 오두막의 고독을 즐긴다.

spatial.en: relating to the position, size, or shape of things in space

ex.en: The test measures a person's spatial awareness.
ex.ko: 그 검사는 사람의 공간 지각 능력을 측정한다.

spatially.en: with regard to the position or arrangement of things in space

ex.en: The rooms were spatially arranged to maximize natural light.
ex.ko: 그 방들은 자연광을 최대화하도록 공간적으로 배치되었다.

spire.en: a tall, pointed tower on top of a building, especially a church

ex.en: The cathedral's spire could be seen from miles away.
ex.ko: 그 대성당의 첨탑은 수 마일 밖에서도 보였다.

splinter.en: a small, thin, sharp piece of wood, glass, or metal that has broken off

ex.en: She got a splinter in her finger from the old fence.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 낡은 울타리에서 손가락에 가시가 박혔다.

splinter.en: break away from a larger group or organization

ex.en: The party splintered into several smaller factions.
ex.ko: 그 정당은 여러 작은 파벌로 분열되었다.

spontaneity.en: the quality of doing things naturally and without planning

ex.en: Their trip was full of spontaneity and unplanned adventures.
ex.ko: 그들의 여행은 즉흥성과 계획되지 않은 모험으로 가득했다.

spurious.en: appearing to be genuine or true but actually false

ex.en: He made spurious claims about the product's benefits.
ex.ko: 그는 그 제품의 효능에 대해 근거 없는 주장을 했다.

spuriously.en: in a way that is false, even though it may appear genuine

ex.en: The data was spuriously linked to unrelated causes.
ex.ko: 그 데이터는 근거 없이 무관한 원인들과 연결되었다.

squint.en: partly close your eyes when looking at something, often because of bright light

ex.en: She squinted against the bright sunlight.
ex.ko: 그녀는 눈부신 햇빛에 실눈을 떴다.

squirm.en: twist your body about, especially because you are uncomfortable or embarrassed

ex.en: The child squirmed in his seat during the long speech.
ex.ko: 그 아이는 긴 연설 동안 자리에서 꿈틀거렸다.

squirmy.en: tending to squirm; unable to stay still, often due to discomfort

ex.en: The squirmy toddler refused to sit still for the photo.
ex.ko: 그 가만히 있지 못하는 아기는 사진을 찍는 동안 가만히 앉아 있으려 하지 않았다.

stabilize.en: make or become stable and no longer fluctuating

ex.en: Doctors worked to stabilize the patient's condition.
ex.ko: 의사들은 그 환자의 상태를 안정시키기 위해 애썼다.

standing.en: a person's reputation or status within a group or society

ex.en: He has a high standing in the medical community.
ex.ko: 그는 의료계에서 높은 신망을 얻고 있다.

standing.en: in a standing position; done while standing

ex.en: The concert offered only standing room tickets.
ex.ko: 그 콘서트는 입석 표만 제공했다.

standing.en: existing permanently, not just for a particular occasion

ex.en: The council set up a standing committee to review the policy.
ex.ko: 그 위원회는 그 정책을 검토할 상설 위원회를 설치했다.
